metadata.description: Symbols absent from Jung/Freud/周公解梦 because they didn't exist yet. Modern life produces dream symbols at speed and the classical symbol library can't catch up. Each has the dreamer's likely contemporary meaning, NOT a Freudian retrofit.

common_variations: phone dies right before sending message
low battery warning that won't go away

psychological_interpretations.modern: Dread of disconnection from one's social/work network. The phone IS the modern external organ — losing power = losing identity-attachment to the world.
psychological_interpretations.anxiety_layer: Often co-occurs with adult work stress + social-media anxiety. Worth noting if recurring.
psychological_interpretations.freudian_retrofit: Castration anxiety modernized — loss of the extension organ

follow_up_questions: What were you trying to do when the battery died?
Did the phone ever come back on?
How did your body feel — was it your phone or felt like part of you?
symbols.ai_application: Recurring phone-dying dreams in adults 25-45 → suggest digital wellbeing reflection.

psychological_interpretations.modern: Disconnection anxiety. Cannot reach help, cannot complete urgent task. Surfaces especially in self-employed / freelance / always-on workers.

symbols.video_prompt_keywords: no signal anxiety, dead zone, frantic refresh, missing bars, can't reach anyone

common_variations: scrolling but each post is the same
scrolling and your finger won't stop

psychological_interpretations.modern: Attention dysregulation surfaced as dream content. The dreaming brain reproduces the doom-scroll trance. Often co-presents with insomnia or screen-time issues.

symbols.ai_application: Co-present with sleep complaints → suggest screen-time / sleep hygiene.

psychological_interpretations.modern: Identity-fragmentation anxiety in the AI era. The 'shadow self' Jungian concept finds a new vehicle: the AI version of you doing things you didn't do.
psychological_interpretations.ai_layer: Frequency rising 2023+. Track as emerging cultural symbol.

follow_up_questions: Was the deepfake doing something specifically harmful?
Did anyone believe it was you?
How did you try to prove it wasn't you?

psychological_interpretations.modern: Update of the classic 'naked in public' dream — but now the public is digitally summoned and you can't escape gracefully. The unmute / camera-on / leave-meeting buttons add control-loss layers.
Specific WFH-era nightmare: showing co-workers something private. Vector for shame about hidden self-aspects.
Communication-control anxiety — the device speaking for you, often saying what you secretly meant or feared meaning.
Existential shadow of the AI-augmented life. Often the AI takes over a creative role the dreamer values — touches identity / replacement fears.

common_variations: notifications stacking faster than you can dismiss

psychological_interpretations.modern: Cognitive load made physical. Often signals burnout / response-burden overflow.

common_variations: delivery driver is someone you know

psychological_interpretations.modern: Misalignment between desire and delivery — what you ordered (figuratively from life) isn't what arrives.
Loss of access to your own digital identity. Often a metaphor for self-estrangement.
psychological_interpretations.freudian_retrofit: Modernized 'finding the right key' — gates to one's own selfhood.

psychological_interpretations.modern: China-specific — the gaotie is the daily metaphor for life pace. Missing it = falling behind. Empty carriage = isolation amid speed.
Loss of agency over your own movement — someone else routes your life.
Sudden bestowal — but is the amount what you expected? Symbol of social-money-bonding mediated by app.
Inability to participate in the modern transactional world. China-specific high-anxiety symbol since QR is required for everything.
Modern abandonment dream — the digital expulsion. Often surfaces relationship anxieties without naming the actual person.
Loss of narrative control — your worst moment becomes everyone's content. Specific to creator-economy era.
Gamified-life anxiety. Often signals over-investment in habit metrics over wellbeing.
Surveillance-era panopticon dream. Watcher you cannot reach back to.
The system doesn't recognize you = you don't fully exist in the system. Identity-validation anxiety.

symbols.name_en: Elevator to a Floor That Doesn't Exist

psychological_interpretations.modern_liminal: Where the workplace meets the dream-architecture sense of place. Office-tower dreams take on liminal quality.
psychological_interpretations.jungian: The Self has a basement / extra room you don't know about.

symbols.video_prompt_keywords: elevator opens to wrong floor, button for floor that doesn't exist, mirrored elevator ceiling, between-floors limbo
